concerns.text: Task T006 defines the physics-based lag formula as `L_phys = (k * 6371) / Vsw_mean / k`, which contains a placeholder variable 'k' and a redundant division. This contradicts spec.md FR-012 which explicitly mandates `L_phys = 6371 / Vsw_mean`. This is a semantic weakening of the requirement, as the task deliverable will not satisfy the verified formula.

Task T011 instructs a sweep of thresholds `T ∈ {high, medium, low}` km/s. Spec.md FR-007 and SC-003 explicitly mandate specific numeric thresholds `T ∈ {400, 500, 600} km s⁻¹`. Using qualitative labels instead of the required numeric values violates the acceptance criteria and drops the specific constraint defined in the spec.

Task T028 describes the sensitivity table deliverable as `T ∈ {, 500, 600} km/s`, missing the lower bound (400) required by spec.md FR-007. This partial implementation fails to satisfy the full acceptance criterion for the sensitivity analysis.

Task T015 conditions the inclusion of the FR-013 narrative note on the permutation test 'successfully executed'. Spec.md FR-013 requires the note to be included in the output report as a standard documentation of the method. Conditional inclusion risks omitting a mandatory requirement if the test fails or is skipped, violating the 'System MUST include' constraint.

Task T003 defines config constants using malformed placeholders (`{{claim:c_f9c59f03}}`, `{{claim:c_255f7...}}`) instead of the specific numeric values required by spec.md FR-005 (10,000 permutations) and FR-006 (1,000 bootstrap iterations). This prevents the configuration from satisfying the explicit iteration counts mandated by the spec.

Task T037 verification step references `{{claim:c_e52f93ed}}` and `pi` instead of the specific numeric tolerance (±1 min) required by spec.md US-2 Independent Test. This makes the test assertion non-deterministic and fails to verify the specific constraint of the optimal lag accuracy.
